provision.title: Set up your weather station
provision.description: Home Assistant is looking for the console's setup network **{ap}** on a spare Wi-Fi radio. Put the console in AP mode (hold the Wi-Fi/Sensor button ~6 s until 'AP' shows), then scan again.

setup.title: Connect your weather station
setup.description: Connected to the console's setup network **{ap}**. Choose the Wi-Fi it should join (must be 2.4 GHz and on the same network as Home Assistant) and confirm the details. Home Assistant configures the console, then waits to confirm it comes online — no IP address needed.

retry.title: Couldn't finish setup
retry.description:
{reason}

Make sure the console still shows **AP** on its screen (hold the **Wi-Fi/Sensor** button ~6 s until it does), then continue to reconnect and re-enter the details.

progress.verify: Waiting for the console to reboot, join **{ssid}**, and reach Home Assistant. This can take up to a minute…

error.ap_not_found: Couldn't find the console's setup network. Put it in AP mode and scan again.
error.ap_join_failed: Found the console's network but couldn't connect to it. Try again.

abort.already_configured: This weather station is already configured.
abort.console_unreachable:
The console joined **{ssid}** and left setup mode, but it can't reach Home Assistant — so it can't send data. **{ssid}** is probably on a different network or VLAN than Home Assistant (common for 'IoT' or 'Guest' Wi-Fi).

To fix it:
1. On the console, hold the **Wi-Fi/Sensor** button ~6 s until **AP** shows.
2. Start setup again and choose a 2.4 GHz network on the **same** network as Home Assistant (Home Assistant is at **{ha_ip}**).

Nothing was changed in Home Assistant.

provision.title: Recover console
provision.description: Put the console in AP mode (hold the Wi-Fi/Sensor button ~6 s until 'AP' shows). Home Assistant will look for its setup network **{ap}** on a spare Wi-Fi radio.

pick.title: Choose the console's Wi-Fi
pick.description: Found the console's setup network **{ap}**. Pick the 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi it should join (must be on the same network as Home Assistant) and enter its password. Home Assistant will provision it, then wait to confirm it comes back online.

retry.title: Couldn't finish recovery
retry.description:
{reason}

Make sure the console still shows **AP** on its screen (hold the **Wi-Fi/Sensor** button ~6 s until it does), then continue to try again.

progress.verify: Waiting for the console to reboot, join **{ssid}**, and reach Home Assistant. This can take up to a minute…

error.ap_not_found: Didn't see the console's setup network yet. Put it in AP mode and scan again.

abort.provision_done: The console reached Home Assistant on **{ssid}** and data has resumed. All set.
abort.console_unreachable:
The console joined **{ssid}** and left setup mode, but it can't reach Home Assistant — so it can't send data. **{ssid}** is probably on a different network or VLAN than Home Assistant (common for 'IoT' or 'Guest' Wi-Fi).

To fix it:
1. On the console, hold the **Wi-Fi/Sensor** button ~6 s until **AP** shows.
2. Run Recover again and choose a 2.4 GHz network on the **same** network as Home Assistant (Home Assistant is at **{ha_ip}**).

Your existing configuration was left unchanged.

reapply_console_settings.name: Re-apply console settings
reapply_console_settings.description: Re-write the console's Custom Server settings so it points back at Home Assistant.
